evidence: Docstring asks for a composite number m with n + f(n) < m < n + p(m), where p(m) is the least prime factor of m.
verify_note: decl exists w/ research-open attr; QID live, label matches; m.Composite is the literal object of the statement

evidence: The set A consists of n expressible as a sum of distinct proper divisors (pseudoperfect/semiperfect numbers) minimal under divisibility, and the problem asks whether the sum of their reciprocals converges.
verify_note: decl exists; QID live (alias semiperfect number); sum-of-distinct-proper-divisors = pseudoperfect is the defining set A

evidence: The statement is Bloom's [Bl21] 'density conjecture about unit fractions' — every A with harmonic sum > delta log N contains S with sum of reciprocals 1 — the strong density form resolving the Erdős–Graham problem.
verify_note: Q2902746 denotes the coloring form of Erdos-Graham (every finite partition of {2,3,...} has a class with unit fractions summing to 1, Croot 2003); erdos_47 states Bloom's density conjecture, a strictly stronger different statement — not the same conjecture

evidence: The statement is about subsets whose unit fractions (reciprocals) sum to 1; references [Bl21] and [LiSa24] are titled on unit fractions and erdosproblems.com tags it 'unit fractions'.
verify_note: decl exists; QID live; reciprocals/unit fractions summing to 1 are the whole content of the statement

evidence: Docstring asks 'Are there any odd weird numbers?', the statement quantifying over n.Weird.
verify_note: decl erdos_470.parts.i exists w/ research-open attr; n.Weird is the statement's predicate

evidence: Docstring asks 'Are there infinitely many primitive weird numbers?', primitive weird being weird numbers with no weird proper divisor.
verify_note: decl erdos_470.parts.ii exists; PrimitiveWeird is defined from n.Weird; load-bearing

evidence: Docstring: Melfi's result is conditional on p_{n+1} - p_n < sqrt(p_n)/10, 'well-known conjectures concerning prime gaps'.
verify_note: decl exists w/ research-solved attr; hypothesis is literally a prime-gap bound primeGap n < sqrt(p_n)/10

evidence: Docstring: 'This is the Erdős-Heilbronn inequality, proved by Dias da Silva and Hamidoune' — |A+^A| >= min(2|A|-3, p) for A in F_p.
verify_note: decl exists; QID live, label exact; |A+^A| >= min(2|A|-3,p) over F_p IS the Erdos-Heilbronn conjecture statement

evidence: The statement bounds the cardinality of A.restrictedSumset, the restricted sumset A+^A = {a+b : a != b in A}.
verify_note: decl exists; A.restrictedSumset is the central object of the statement

evidence: The statement asks whether for all k > 1 there are infinitely many n with 2^n = k (mod n), a congruence condition on n.
verify_note: decl exists; the statement is the congruence 2^n = k (mod n) via Nat.ModEq — the modular condition is the entire content, though the concept is broad

evidence: Docstring asks whether there are infinitely many n, m with phi(n) = sigma(m), phi being Euler's totient (n.totient in the statement).
verify_note: decl exists; n.totient appears directly in the statement

evidence: The statement equates phi(n) with the sum-of-divisors function sigma(m) (σ 1 m via ArithmeticFunction.sigma).
verify_note: decl exists; sigma 1 m (ArithmeticFunction.sigma) is the other side of the equation; QID label 'divisor sum function' matches

evidence: Chung and Graham's constant c = 1 + sum 1/F_{2k} 'where F_m is the mth Fibonacci number' per the docstring.
verify_note: decl erdos_480.variants.chung_graham exists w/ research-solved attr; constant c = 1 + sum 1/F_{2k} is defined via Nat.fib

evidence: The best-possible statement uses the same Fibonacci-defined constant c = 1 + sum 1/F_{2k}.
verify_note: decl erdos_480.variants.chung_graham_best_possible exists; same Fibonacci-defined constant is load-bearing
